content:
Are there examples around where handles to an instance of a subsystem are stored somewhere in driver state with the help of safe abstractions? I was wondering about something like i2c_client* that one would probably put into dev_get_drvdata for a C module. I would be interested into how a safe wrapper for something like this could look like...

I tried poking in the current https://github.com/Rust-for-Linux/linux/tree/rust tree, but there the only thing I found were device handles. But those only get used for the dev_dbg!() macros and nothing else. Does anyone have an idea where I could get some inspiration for this?

I found an example at https://github.com/Rust-for-Linux/linux/pull/264/files#diff-0f406a03a50b38ab2ae05ad680af750e7430fd61f1e54e7024e52c7187cb89d0R16. But I _think_ that that one is flawed (left a comment inline).
